items.headline: NBA Game 3 Highlights: Lakers 112, Rockets 108 (OT)
items.blurb: NBA Game 3 Highlights: Lakers 112, Rockets 108 (OT) — LeBron James led the way with 29 points and 13 rebounds as the Los Angeles Lakers edged the Houston Rockets 112-108 in overtime to take a 3-0 series lead. Marcus Smart also added, 21 points, 10 assists and 5 steals in the win.

items.headline: Jayson Tatum, Jaylen Brown help Celtics take Game 3 vs. 76ers
items.blurb: Jayson Tatum, Jaylen Brown help Celtics take Game 3 vs. 76ers — Thanks to a 108-100 win on Friday night, the Boston Celtics were able to take a 2-1 series lead over the Philadelphia 76ers in their first-round Eastern Conference matchup.

items.blurb: Lakers’ James comes up with steal, drains game-tying three to force OT — Watch as Los Angeles Lakers star LeBron James pokes the ball out for a steal, then sinks a game-tying three forcing overtime in a wild sequence vs. the Houston Rockets.

items.headline: LeBron James' big moments lifting Lakers to commanding series lead vs. Rockets
items.blurb: LeBron James' big moments lifting Lakers to commanding series lead vs. Rockets — A series many thought would go one way due to key injuries has gone the completely other direction. With their come-from-behind Game 3 win on Friday night, it's the Los Angeles Lakers who have a commanding 3-0 series lead over the Houston Rockets.

items.blurb: NBA Game 3 Highlights: Celtics 108, 76ers 100 — Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown scored 25 apiece as Boston Celtics beat Philadelphia 76ers 108-100 to take a 2-1 series lead.

items.blurb: Joel Embiid Ruled Out Of Game 3 After Making Progress In Return From Appendectomy — Joel Embiid has made progress in his return from an emergency appendectomy, but was ruled out of Game 3 as the Philadelphia 76ers host the Boston Celtics. items.headline: Nickeil Alexander-Walker Named NBA's Most Improved Player
items.blurb: Nickeil Alexander-Walker Named NBA's Most Improved Player — Nickeil Alexander-Walker has been named the NBA's Most Improved Player . Alexander-Walker averaged 20.8 points in his first season with the Atlanta Hawks after averaging 9.4 points per game last season with the Minnesota Timberwolves. items.headline: Kevin Durant Out For Game 3 With Sprained Right Ankle
items.blurb: Kevin Durant Out For Game 3 With Sprained Right Ankle — Kevin Durant has been ruled out by the Houston Rockets for their critical Game 3 hosting the Los Angeles Lakers due to a sprained right ankle. Durant missed Game 1 due to a knee contusion and suffered the ankle injury in Game 2. Durant has been experiencing swelling in his left ankle since the injury. items.headline: Star freshman Darryn Peterson, Cameron Boozer declare for NBA draft
items.blurb: Star freshman Darryn Peterson, Cameron Boozer declare for NBA draft — Kansas freshman Darryn Peterson declared for the NBA draft on Friday, just as nearly everyone had expected he would ever since his arrival on campus, and the high-scoring guard figures to be among the first three players selected in June.

items.blurb: Michigan Lands 7-Foot-2 Transfer Moustapha Thiam From Cincinnati — Cincinnati transfer center Moustapha Thiam, rated the No. 11 overall player in ESPN's transfer portal rankings, has committed to Michigan, he told ESPN's Shams Charania on Friday. items.blurb: Kevin Durant Eyes NBA Europe Ownership Through PSG Investment — Houston Rockets forward Kevin Durant holds a minority stake in Paris Saint-Germain, whose ownership group is actively pursuing a franchise in NBA Europe, the projected 2027-28 international league. items.blurb: CAA's Austin Brown Decides Not To Pursue Bulls' President Of Basketball Job — CAA agent Austin Brown has decided not to pursue the Chicago Bulls' head of basketball operations position. Brown was the only candidate to not currently work in an NBA front office. items.headline: Amen Thompson: Elite two-way performance
items.blurb: Amen Thompson: Elite two-way performance — Thompson totaled 26 points (8-14 FG, 0-1 3Pt, 10-13 FT), 11 rebounds, four assists, three blocks and three steals over 48 minutes during Friday's 112-108 overtime loss to the Lakers in Game 3 of the first round of the Western Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Alperen Sengun: Huge double-double in loss
items.blurb: Alperen Sengun: Huge double-double in loss — Sengun supplied 33 points (15-27 FG, 1-5 3Pt, 2-3 FT), 16 rebounds, six assists, three steals and one block across 47 minutes in Friday's 112-108 overtime loss to the Lakers in Game 3 of the first round of the playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: LeBron James: Stuffs stat sheet in win
items.blurb: LeBron James: Stuffs stat sheet in win — James finished Friday's 112-108 overtime win over the Rockets in Game 3 of the first round of the playoffs with 29 points (10-22 FG, 4-9 3Pt, 5-6 FT), 13 rebounds, six assists, three steals and one block in 45 minutes. items.blurb: Tyrese Maxey: Game-high 31 points in loss — Maxey amassed 31 points (12-31 FG, 5-13 3Pt, 2-2 FT), six assists, four rebounds, two blocks and one steal across 42 minutes in Friday's 108-100 Game 3 loss to the Celtics in the first round of the playoffs. items.blurb: Mikal Bridges Benched For Most Of Second Half In Knicks' Game 3 Loss At Hawks — Mikal Bridges logged a career playoff low of just 21 minutes as the New York Knicks dropped Game 3 to the Atlanta Hawks 109-108 Thursday, falling behind 2-1 in the first-round series. items.headline: Jaylen Brown: Well-rounded performance in win
items.blurb: Jaylen Brown: Well-rounded performance in win — Brown supplied 25 points (9-16 FG, 1-2 3Pt, 6-6 FT), seven rebounds, four assists, three blocks and one steal across 40 minutes in Friday's 108-100 Game 3 win over the 76ers in the first round of the playoffs. items.blurb: Wolves Win Game 3 Over Nuggets, Hold Nikola Jokic To Worst Shooting Night Of Playoff Career — Nikola Jokic endured the worst shooting night of his playoff career Thursday, connecting on just 7 of 26 field goal attempts as the Minnesota Timberwolves defeated the Denver Nuggets 113-96 to claim a 2-1 first-round series lead. RJ Barrett and Scottie Barnes each finished with 33 points in the game, a playoff career-high for both players.
